tr.td: Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: All measured laboratory values are within normal limits for this 84-year-old female patient. There is no evidence of anemia, renal dysfunction, electrolyte imbalance, dyslipidemia, or abnormal glucose metabolism. Urinary microalbumin/creatinine ratio is normal, indicating no sign of early kidney damage. Overall, the laboratory findings are unremarkable and do not suggest any acute or chronic pathology at this time.

display.value: Neuropathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

tr.td: Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
